defaults.global_prompt: CRITICAL RULES: 1) Do NOT run git reset/rollback/revert. 2) Maintain current UI design theme. 3) Read orchestrator/runner/AGENT_SPEC.md for full specification before starting work.

workers.goal: Read orchestrator/runner/AGENT_SPEC.md first. Then implement: (1) Separate app tabs into Operations tab and Monitor tab. Operations tab has server control + equipment ON/OFF cards + log buttons. Monitor tab has existing graphs/timeline/detailed status. (2) Implement server state machine OFF->STARTING->ON->STOPPING->OFF/ERROR in controller. Add progress bar and step label for transitions. (3) Prevent duplicate clicks during server actions. (4) On app exit, stop all web servers and collectors. (5) Equipment online/offline detection: create single function using connection_state + latest_sample_age. No false ON display for offline equipment. After changes, run: python -m py_compile app.py runtime/controller.py diagnostics/runtime_info_dialog.py
done_when: Operations and Monitor tabs are separate
Server state machine is implemented with progress UI
App exit cleans up all processes
Single is_equipment_online() function exists
py_compile passes on all changed files

workers.goal: Read orchestrator/runner/AGENT_SPEC.md first. Then implement: (1) Operations tab UI: remove all combo boxes, keep slide toggle buttons for server and equipment. Equipment cards show: name, online/offline status, slide toggle, log button. (2) Layout 8 cards per row on FHD (1920x1080). Fix first-launch 1-column bug from showEvent timing. (3) Header row: add function buttons (Home, Settings, Admin, server status badge, notifications, user menu). Server status badge shows STARTING/STOPPING/ERROR/ON/OFF with live feedback. Header linked to operations tab. (4) QSS bugfix only: text clipping, button alignment, badge colors. Online badge=green, offline badge=gray. Do NOT change theme. After changes run: python -m py_compile diagnostics/runtime_info_dialog.py runtime/controller.py
done_when: No combo boxes in operations tab
Equipment cards with slide toggle and log button
8 cards per row on FHD
Header has function buttons and server status badge
QSS bugs fixed without theme change

workers.goal: Read orchestrator/runner/AGENT_SPEC.md first. Then implement: (1) Per-equipment log viewer as separate window: equipment selection combo in log window (not operations tab), single-instance enforcement (bring to front if already open), refresh button, load recent N lines for large logs without UI freeze. (2) Server settings validation: port 1-65535, paths must exist or be creatable, intervals positive integers in reasonable range. Clear error messages on failure. (3) --fleet-startup option simplified to start|skip only. Update help text. (4) server_control_gui.py: mark as deprecated auxiliary tool, disable from main flow. After changes run: python -m py_compile on changed files
done_when: Log viewer opens as separate single-instance window
Settings validation with clear error messages
--fleet-startup accepts only start|skip
server_control_gui marked deprecated

workers.goal: Read orchestrator/runner/AGENT_SPEC.md first. Then rebuild the web interface: (1) Read web/static/design_reference.html as the target design. (2) Update web/static/index.html and web/static/styles.css to match the reference design: header with logo+nav+server status+notifications+user menu, KPI donut cards (4 columns), machine cards with 24h timeline bars, footer with system health. (3) Match colors exactly: corpBlue=#2D4B9B, corpRed=#E31E24, surface=#F8FAFC, statusGreen=#10B981, statusGray=#94A3B8, statusOrange=#F59E0B, statusRed=#EF4444. (4) Use Inter font family. (5) Responsive layout (mobile/desktop). (6) Keep existing web API data binding in app.js working. Web is admin/view/analysis only, no server control duplication.
done_when: Web matches design_reference.html layout and colors
Header, KPI cards, machine cards, timeline, footer implemented
Responsive layout works
Existing API data binding preserved

workers.goal: Read orchestrator/runner/AGENT_SPEC.md first. Then validate all changes: (1) Run python -m py_compile on ALL .py files in the repo. Report any syntax errors. (2) Verify app entry point (python -m machining_monitor_server or app.py) can be imported without errors. (3) Code review the full lifecycle: app start -> server ON -> equipment ON/OFF -> log window -> monitor tab -> server OFF -> app exit. Document if each step is implementable from the code. (4) Check that web/static/index.html structure matches design_reference.html. (5) Write results to orchestrator/results.md under [AGENT-T5] section and orchestrator/status_report.md.
done_when: All .py files pass py_compile
App entry point imports successfully
Lifecycle code review documented
Web design match verified
Results written to results.md and status_report.md
